= Configuring Network Services

This guide explains how to configure network services on {productname}. You will learn about setting up DHCP, DNS, and managing firewall rules using YaST and command-line tools.

== Prerequisites

Before configuring network services, ensure that:

* Your system has network connectivity
* You have administrator privileges
* The required packages are installed

== Configuring DHCP

Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) automatically assigns IP addresses to devices on your network. To configure a DHCP server:

. Install the DHCP server package
. Edit the `/etc/dhcpd.conf` configuration file
. Define your network ranges and options
. Start and enable the DHCP service

== Managing DNS

Domain Name System (DNS) translates domain names to IP addresses. You can configure DNS using:

* YaST network configuration module
* Direct editing of `/etc/resolv.conf`
* NetworkManager for desktop systems

== Firewall Configuration

The built-in firewall protects your system from unauthorized access. Key concepts include:

* Zones (trusted, public, dmz)
* Services and ports
* Rich rules for advanced filtering

Use `firewalld` or YaST to manage firewall settings.

== Troubleshooting

Common issues and solutions:

* Check service status with `systemctl`
* Review logs in `/var/log/messages`
* Verify network interface configuration
* Test connectivity with `ping` and `traceroute`

== Additional Resources

For more information, consult the {productname} documentation and community forums.
